final_message: That's a great question! 🌟 Let me think about the best way to explain this. Can you tell me what part is most confusing? We'll figure it out together! 💪
current_section_title: Choosing the Right Unit: Metres or Centimetres
current_section_content: We measure length using different units depending on how big or small the object is. Centimetres (cm) are used for smaller objects like a mobile phone or a handkerchief, while metres (m) are used for larger things like the height of a building or the length of an elephant's trunk. Choosing the right unit makes measurement easier and more sensible. For example, saying an elephant's trunk is 2 m long is much clearer than saying it is 200 cm.
